name: Cepheid1b
description: Red eFRET voltage indicator built by inserting the bright RFP donor mScarlet-I1.4 into the first extracellular loop of Ace(D81C) rhodopsin; the brighter (but less photostable) member of the Cepheid1 pair.

spectrum.name: Cepheid1b
spectrum.note: Cepheid1b uses the RFP donor mScarlet-I1.4 (an engineered brighter variant of mScarlet-I) inserted into ECL1 of AceD81C rhodopsin. Excitation max 570 nm, emission max 600 nm per the paper's mScarlet-I1.4 spectra (Fig. S2; Table S5). Custom excitation/emission curves are the mScarlet-I/mScarlet-I1.4 donor profile (asymmetric emission with a long red tail and a vibronic excitation shoulder).

kinetics.note: Cepheid1b in HEK293T cells (room temperature, 1058 Hz frame rate). Fig. S7B is a dual-axis bar chart: depolarization (-70 to +30 mV) is read on the LEFT red axis (0-2 ms) and repolarization (+30 to -70 mV) on the RIGHT blue axis (0-3 ms). Pixel-calibrated read: depol t½ ≈ 1.88 ms (the Solaris paper, same lab, confirms depolarizing τ1/2 = 1.9 ± 0.1 ms) and repol t½ ≈ 3.46 ms (the repol bar extends above the right-axis '3' line). Converted to time constants via τ = t½/ln2 = 1.443·t½ → on 2.71 ms, off 4.99 ms. (A previous read of off=4.17 ms came from mis-applying a 1.5× left:right axis ratio; the true ratio is ~1.69×.)

Cross-measured in the Solaris paper (Yang et al. 2024, bioRxiv) as a comparator: Cepheid1b depolarizing-step response half-time τ1/2 = 1.9 ± 0.1 ms in HEK293T cells at room temperature under 561 nm wide-field (1P) illumination, camera frame rate 1058 Hz (n = 5 cells). Converted to a time constant via τ = t½/ln2 = 1.443·t½ → on 2.74 ms. Only the depolarizing (on) τ is reported for Cepheid1b in this paper (the 1.5 ± 0.2 ms value in the same sentence is Cepheid1s). Consistent with the origin-paper Fig. S7B depol read (2.71 ms).

dynamicRangeData.note: Cepheid1b sensitivity per 100 mV step (-70 to +30 mV) in HEK293T cells under whole-cell voltage clamp (widefield 1P, 1058 Hz); Fig. S7A bar mean ≈31%. The main text/Fig. 1B value of -32.9 ± 1.0% (n = 6 cells) is the wider -70 to +40 mV (110 mV) step and is overstated for a 100 mV window.

sensitivityData.note: Cepheid1b -12.1 ± 0.8% per AP in cultured rat hippocampal neurons (current clamp, widefield 1P imaging at 484 Hz)

apWidthData.sample: cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Optical single-AP FWHM derived by digitizing the averaged Cepheid1b optical fluorescence waveform in Fig. 1D (maroon ΔF/F trace vs time; 20-ms scale bar). Re-digitized at 600 dpi (2026-07-04, targeted re-check): scale bar = 91.6 px sub-pixel = 20 ms (4.578 px/ms); maroon center-line traced column-by-column; baseline = 418.5 px (flat pre-spike segment), peak = 252 px (max upward deflection, not clipped), half-max level y = 335.2 px; sub-pixel half-max crossings at rise x=296.6 and fall x=328.4 → span 31.8 px → FWHM 6.95 ms → 7.0 ms (±0.5 ms digitization uncertainty). This supersedes prior reads of 8.4 ms (low-res PMC JPEG, lower-edge crossing) and 7.8 ms (a mutually-inconsistent 300-dpi read requiring scale bar 42-43 px AND FWHM 16.5-17.1 px simultaneously); the 600-dpi scale bar is 45.8 px/300 dpi and the half-max width is 15.9 px/300 dpi, both consistent, giving 7.0 ms. Physically consistent with τoff ~5 ms. Recorded in current-clamp cultured hippocampal neurons under widefield 1P imaging at 484 Hz (Methods); the frame period (~2.1 ms) is well below the width, so the value is kinetics-dominated. No numeric FWHM is stated in the paper; this is plot-derived.

brightnessData.note: Cepheid1b average photon count ~960 a.u. vs VARNAM ~580 a.u. per cell (Fig. S5)

photostabilityData.note: Cepheid1b (mScarlet-I1.4 donor) normalized intensity ≈38% at 13 min under 1.59 W/cm² (= 15.9 mW/mm²) 561 nm widefield illumination (Fig. S6A). Cepheid1b is the brighter but less photostable variant; the ~78% remaining (top curve in Fig. S6A) belongs to Cepheid1s (mRuby4), which the main text describes as having a half-photobleach time >13 min.

photobleach.note: Cepheid1b (mScarlet-I1.4 donor) photobleaching digitized from Fig. S6A under 1.59 W/cm² (= 15.9 mW/mm²) 561 nm wide-field illumination (HEK293T, n = 6). HSV colour-separated from the 5-curve panel (Cepheid1b = gold-olive curve, ending ≈38% at 13 min). Bi-exponential fit; model-free t₇₅% ≈ 108 s. Cepheid1b is the brighter but less photostable member of the pair (cf. Cepheid1s, the top curve). Companion curves in this figure (Cepheid1s, VARNAM, VARNAM2) cross-overlay via the shared source + Fig. S6A.

researchPapers.title: Bright and sensitive red voltage indicators for imaging action potentials in brain slices and pancreatic islets

researchPapers.title: Solaris: a panel of bright and sensitive hybrid voltage indicators for imaging membrane potential in cultured neurons
researchPapers.authors: Yang J, Zhu S, Yang L, Peng L, Han Y, Hayward RF, Park P, Hu D, Cohen AE, Zou P
researchPapers.sample: HEK293T cells, in vitro (Cepheid1b measured as a comparator: voltage-clamp response half-time τ1/2 = 1.9 ± 0.1 ms at 1058 Hz)
